body:
Hey Em,

Just a heads-up - I pulled up AutoFlow this morning before unlocking the bays and noticed the Tuesday slot for the Kowalski fleet service is showing unconfirmed. That's a four-vehicle block and I need Miguel and Omar on it from open, so it can't be a surprise when they walk in.

Can you check on the system side and make sure it's locked in? Also the Wednesday afternoon bay two slot looks like it got double-booked somehow - Reyes transmission and a walk-in oil change both sitting in the same window. That needs sorting before I get there tomorrow.

I've got parts deliveries coming in Monday and Thursday so the schedule being clean actually matters this week. Don't need the front desk guessing.

Dave

Hey Dave,

Got it - I pulled up AutoFlow and confirmed the Kowalski block for Tuesday. It was sitting in a pending state because the contact email bounced when the reminder went out. I manually confirmed it and flagged Miguel and Omar on the job notes, so they'll see it first thing.

The Wednesday conflict in bay two was a system overlap from a walk-in that got entered without checking availability. I moved the oil change to bay three at 2:30 - should give you enough buffer before close. Notes are updated.

Schedule looks clean through the rest of the week now. I'll keep an eye on it Monday morning before your parts deliveries land so nothing piles up on you.

Emily

Hey Em,

Stopped by Seven Oaks this morning before the bay doors opened - figured I'd grab my usual and get ahead of the parts delivery confirmations before the phones started. You mentioned last week you wanted to try that place, so heads up: the iced Americano is legit. Nothing fancy, just good coffee. They pull a decent shot and don't drown it.

They also had those little cardamom cookies at the counter. I didn't buy one. I'm telling you about them anyway.

Let me know if you want to swing by sometime. I'm usually there early enough that it's still quiet.

Dave

Dave,

Okay you had me at 'decent shot' and then completely lost me at mentioning the cardamom cookie and then NOT buying it. What is wrong with you. That's practically a crime.

I've been looking for a new coffee spot since that place on Pelham started burning their beans - honestly Seven Oaks sounds perfect. Maybe I can meet you there one morning before you head into the shop? I'm usually up early anyway and I could use an excuse to actually leave the house before noon on a weekday.

Also - are you coaching Eagles practice tonight? Kevin mentioned the FIFA rematch is happening after. Tell him I said he's going down (not that he'll listen).

Em

Em,

The cookie thing was a conscious decision. I'm not explaining myself.

Yeah, come by any morning - I'm usually there by 7:15, sometimes earlier if the AutoFlow schedule is a mess and I need to think before I start talking to people. Just text me the night before and I'll make sure I'm not rushing.

Practice is at Hemlock at five, equipment bag is already in the truck. Kevin's going to lose at FIFA regardless of what you tell him - he celebrates too early every single time and I just wait him out. It's almost too easy at this point.

Seven Oaks Tuesday? I'll even let you order the cookie.

Dave

Hey Em,

Can you pull up what's still outstanding on the parts side for this week? I checked AutoFlow before I left this morning and a couple of line items are showing "pending" with no ETA - the front caliper set for the Tahoe on bay three and the alternator we ordered for Mrs. Keller's Accord. Both jobs are scheduled Thursday and I'd rather know now if we're going to have a problem.

Also, if the Dorman shipment is coming in today, make sure someone's around to sign for it - last time it sat on the dock for two hours because nobody flagged it.

Appreciate it.
David

David,

Got your message. Pulled the order history - the caliper set shipped yesterday from the Detroit warehouse, estimated delivery tomorrow by noon, so you should be clear for Thursday. The alternator is the one to watch: the supplier site shows it left their facility Monday but tracking hasn't updated since. I called and left a message; waiting to hear back.

Dorman delivery is confirmed for this afternoon between 2 and 4. I flagged it with the front desk so someone will be there to receive it and check the packing list against the PO. I'll email you the confirmation once it's signed in.

Let me know if you need anything else before Thursday.
Emily

Em,

Thanks. If the alternator tracking doesn't update by end of day, go ahead and call their parts line directly - the website's usually a day behind and it's not worth guessing on it. I've got the Keller job penciled in for 10 AM Thursday and she's already rescheduled once, so I'd rather source a backup from AutoZone than leave her hanging again.

Also, can you double-check the Dorman invoice when it comes in? Last shipment they shorted us two sets of brake pads and we didn't catch it until we went to pull the stock. Just want to make sure the count matches before I sign off on payment.

David

David,

Just heard back from the supplier - alternator is on the truck and will deliver tomorrow morning with the rest of the Thursday inbounds. They confirmed the part number matches, so you're good. No need for the AutoZone backup.

Dorman came in at 3:15. I checked the packing list against the PO line by line - everything's accounted for, including both brake pad sets. Invoice matches. I've filed the confirmation and flagged it ready for payment approval whenever you want to sign off.

You should be set for the week. I'll keep an eye on anything that comes in overnight and let you know if anything changes.
Emily

Hey Em,

Quick one - can you swing the equipment bag by the shop before five? Eagles practice is at Hemlock Park tonight and I'll be under a Silverado most of the afternoon. If you drop it here I can load it straight into the truck when I wrap up.

Bag should be in the mudroom closet, bottom shelf. Orange Eagles duffel. Make sure the mesh ball bag is stuffed inside - last time half the kids were kicking a single cone around for ten minutes while I dug through the trunk. Not my finest coaching moment.

Let me know if you can't make it and I'll figure something out.

Dave

Dave,

Yes, I can bring it. I've got a thing in Dearborn Heights until around 3:30 so I'll head straight to the shop after - should be there by 4:15, 4:30 at the latest. That gives you plenty of buffer before you need to leave.

I checked the mudroom - orange duffel is there and the ball bag is already inside. Looks like someone actually remembered to pack it after last week, so you're ahead of schedule for once.

One thing: the pump was sitting on the shelf next to it. Looked a little busted. Want me to grab it anyway or leave it?

Em

Hey Em,

Just wanted to give you a heads-up - Kevin and I finally got that FIFA session in last night. Online co-op, so no excuses about controller lag when he loses. Kid's actually gotten pretty sharp. Had me scrambling a couple times, which I'll deny if you repeat it.

We played later than I planned. Had Eagles practice run long at Hemlock Park and then a parts delivery showed up right as I was locking up the shop, so by the time I got on it was almost 9. Kevin was already in the lobby waiting. Typical.

Tell him good game if you talk to him before I do. And tell him not to get comfortable.

David

Ha, I already heard about it from him - he texted me literally right after saying he 'almost had you.' I'm choosing to believe that means you won.

Glad you guys finally made it happen. He's been looking forward to it all week, honestly. You know how he gets when he's got something to look forward to. Also sounds like your Tuesdays are absolutely unhinged - Eagles practice, parts deliveries, and then online FIFA at 9pm? When do you sleep?

Same time next week, or is he going to have to wait another month?

Emily

Emily,

'Almost had me' is generous on his part but I'll let it stand.

Same time next week works. I'll actually block it off - between the shop schedule and getting the equipment bag sorted for practice, things stack up faster than they should. Easier to just put it on the calendar and treat it like a delivery window. Non-negotiable.

And I sleep fine, thanks for asking. Coffee helps.

Talk soon,
David

Hey Em,

Just wanted to fill you in - Kevin and I finally got our weekly FIFA session in last night. Kid's been grinding ranked matches all month apparently, so I figured I was walking into something ugly. I was right. Lost 3-2 in extra time, which I'm choosing to call a moral victory.

Told him we'd run it back Thursday after I drop the equipment bag off at Hemlock Park for Eagles practice. He's already talking trash about it, so that should tell you something about how last night went.

All good on your end?

David

Ha! Three-two in extra time doesn't sound like a moral victory to me, but I'll let you have it.

Seriously though, it's good you two are keeping that going. Kevin mentioned to me last week he looks forward to it - probably won't say that to your face, obviously, but there it is. Don't let him know I told you or I'll never hear the end of it.

Are you doing anything with Dad and Mom this weekend, or is it all shop and soccer until further notice?

Love,
Emily

Em,

Appreciate you saying that, even if I fully expect Kevin to deny it. Thursday rematch went better - won 4-1. Justice served.

As for the weekend, probably swinging by Mom and Dad's Sunday afternoon after we close up at Hayes Auto. Nothing planned, just figured I'd check in. You know how it is - easier to show up than to coordinate.

You coming down at all? Mom's been asking.

David

A 4-1 win after a 3-2 loss. You're keeping very careful score, David.

I'll try to make Sunday work. I've got some things to sort out Saturday but should be free by afternoon. If you're heading over around 2 or 3, maybe we can overlap. Tell Mom I'm not dodging her, I'm just working on timing.

And tell Kevin his reign is officially over, apparently.

Love,
Emily

Hey Em,

Following up on the Gmail API access you helped me set up last week. Most of it's working fine - emails are pulling through to AutoFlow no problem. The issue I'm running into now is the OAuth token keeps expiring and I have to re-authenticate every couple days. That's manageable when I'm at the shop, but I missed a parts confirmation from Hendrick Supply on Tuesday because the connection dropped overnight.

I think it's something about the refresh token not persisting, but that's about where my expertise ends. You mentioned there was a setting for offline access - is that what I need to look at here?

Thanks,
David

Hi David,

Yes, that's exactly the issue - when the app requests Gmail access, it needs to include 'access_type=offline' in the OAuth scope to receive a refresh token that persists. If that parameter was missing on the initial authorization, the token will expire after an hour or so and won't renew itself.

The fix is straightforward: revoke the current app access in your Google Account settings under Security > Third-party access, then re-authorize AutoFlow with the offline access parameter enabled. Once you do that, the refresh token gets stored and the connection will stay live without you touching it.

If AutoFlow's settings don't expose that option directly, let me know and I can walk you through adding it on the API side. Shouldn't take more than ten minutes - I know you've got enough going on with the shop without chasing auth errors before the bay doors open.

Emily

Hey Em,

Following up on the Google Calendar API thing we talked about last week. AutoFlow handles the shop schedule fine day-to-day, but I keep running into situations where I want to push confirmed appointments straight into my personal Google Calendar without doing it twice. Figured there had to be a way to hook that up.

From what I read, it sounds like I'd need OAuth credentials and a service account or something - honestly got a little lost in the docs after the third page. Is this something you can walk me through, or does it need a developer to set up properly?

No rush on my end. Eagles practice is Thursday so I'm slammed before 17:00, but I'm around after.

David

Hey David,

Good timing - I actually pulled up the notes from our last call before you emailed. The short answer is yes, you can absolutely connect AutoFlow to Google Calendar via the API, and it doesn't require a full developer setup on your end. You'll need a Google Cloud project with the Calendar API enabled, then generate OAuth 2.0 credentials. The service account route is cleaner for server-side stuff, but for a single-user shop scenario, the standard OAuth flow is easier to maintain.

I can send you a step-by-step doc today. The main thing you'll need upfront is access to the Google account you want the appointments to land in, and about 20 minutes to walk through the consent screen setup. Once the credentials are in place, the actual calendar writes are pretty straightforward.

Let me know if you want to do a screen share instead - sometimes easier than reading docs after a long day in the bays.

Emily

Em,

A step-by-step doc works great, send it over. Screen share is fine too if it ends up being easier - just not Thursday, and probably not Friday morning since I've got a parts delivery coming in and Omar and I will be tied up getting everything checked off.

I'm using the same Google account for everything - shop, personal, calendar. One less thing to manage. If it pushes confirmed jobs in there cleanly, it'll save me the double-entry I've been doing every morning before the Americano even kicks in.

Appreciate you looking into it. Let me know what you need from my end to get started.

David
